context: Tests whether the agent follows the refactor-safely protocol: writing characterization tests that pass on the existing code before any structural change, explicitly stating stable behavior, proposing a minimal step-by-step sequence, verifying tests after each step with concrete evidence, and avoiding mixing behavior changes with structural work.

checklist.name: Stable behavior statement
checklist.description: A written statement declaring which behaviors must not change is present before or alongside the refactoring plan — not implied, but explicitly stated

checklist.name: Characterization tests before refactoring
checklist.description: Spec or test file content appears in the process log or workspace before any implementation-level structural change is described — test code is NOT introduced after the new service file

checklist.name: Characterization tests target current code
checklist.description: The characterization tests are written against the existing code (the fat controller or original structure), not the extracted service — they protect existing behavior, not new behavior

checklist.name: Smallest safe sequence proposed
checklist.description: The refactoring plan lists discrete steps in sequence (numbered or ordered), extracting one boundary per step, not a single all-at-once rewrite

checklist.name: Test run evidence per step
checklist.description: The process log contains at least two entries that report concrete test run results (e.g. pass count, zero failures, exit code) at different points in the sequence — NOT just a final result

checklist.name: No behavior mixed with structure
checklist.description: The refactoring does NOT alter application logic (add discount rules, change tax calculation, add validations) in the same commit or step as the structural extraction

checklist.name: Temporary compatibility noted
checklist.description: If the public interface changes (e.g. the controller action delegates to a new class), the process log or plan explicitly identifies any interim shim or compatibility wrapper and states when it will be removed

checklist.name: No forbidden confidence claims
checklist.description: The process log does NOT contain any of the following unsupported claims without accompanying test evidence: 'should work', 'looks correct', 'I'm confident', or 'this is fine'

checklist.name: One boundary extracted per step
checklist.description: Each numbered step in the plan/log touches a single extraction target (e.g. only the pricing logic, or only the notification dispatch) — not multiple unrelated concerns in one step
